item.title: K8 billion lost in illicit forestry activities under investigation
item.description: [Act Now PNG] Estimated K8.78 billion lost through illicit forestry activities and suspected money laundering schemes is now at the centre of four major investigations under Operation Arcadia, a multi-agency crackdown supported by INTERPOL.

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts :Bougainville Landowners demand mining companies halt reopening of copper pit
item.description: [Bougainville News] Traditional landowners are fighting back against plans to reopen an existing Bougainville copper mine which has remained dormant for nearly four decades. During a public event on Bougainville Island last week a warning was declared against the companies who landowners … Continue reading →

item.title: Hotel Review: Citi Boutique Hotel, Port Moresby
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Mary Tao reviews the hotel with 'something for everyone': the Citi Boutique Hotel, located adjacent to the Santos National Football Stadium in Port Moresby. The post Hotel Review: Citi Boutique Hotel, Port Moresby appeared first on Business Advantage PNG .

item.title: USP Entrepreneurial Fair 2026 brings powerful ideas and youthful energy to Laucala campus
item.description: [Education News PNG] The University of the South Pacific (USP) has launched its 2026 Entrepreneurial Fair, bringing together students, academics, and industry partners under the theme “AI, Innovation and The post USP Entrepreneurial Fair 2026 brings powerful ideas and youthful energy to Laucala campus first appeared on

item.title: Eco-Health and Culture, PNG and Timor-Leste Strengthen Ties
item.description: [Inside PNG] Nobel Laureate Dr. José Ramos-Horta visits Port Moresby, backing PNG’s eco-leadership and inviting PNG musicians to Timor-Leste. A new era for Melanesian ties. The post Eco-Health and Culture, PNG and Timor-Leste Strengthen Ties first appeared on Inside PNG . The post Eco-Health and Culture, PNG and

item.title: Fuel Price Drops in PNG Bringing Relief to Consumers Nationwide
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Motorists across Papua New Guinea are seeing relief at fuel stations after the Independent Consumer and Competition Commission (ICCC) confirmed that retail fuel prices have dropped following the National Government’s subsidy intervention. Fuel Price Drops in PNG Bringing Relief to Consumers Nationwi

item.title: Matrilineal & Patrilineal Societies in Papua New Guinea
item.description: [Pawa TV] Papua New Guinea's societal structures include matrilineal and patrilineal systems, influencing descent, land rights, and gender roles, with matrilineal communities empowering women through inheritance and social influence, and patrilineal focusing on male lineage. The post Matrilineal & Patrilineal

item.title: PNG50 Gospel Music Festival set for August
item.description: [PNG Buzz] Jubilee Reset Festival: A Nation Gathers to Worship, Pray, and Reset for the Future As Papua New Guinea approaches its 50th year of Independence, a powerful wave of faith and unity is building across the nation. At the heart of this movement is the Jubilee Reset Gospel Music Festival, taking place o

item.title: PNG PM Marape Apologises Over Fuel Supply Disruptions
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea Prime Minister James Marape has apologised to citizens following fuel shortages and panic buying experienced across parts of the country over the weekend, saying delays within government payment processes contributed to the situation. Mr Marape said the State has now moved to stabil

item.title: Educating ourselves on cancer
item.description: [Post Courier] In any village in Papua New Guinea, the betelnut is found. In the Highlands where it is not grown, the sellers take this fruit or nut all the way to the furthest corners of the country to sell, and it sells well. Betelnut is truly one of PNG’s green gold commodities, apart from coffee. To access thi

item.title: East New Britain residents to benefit from free ENB Savings and Loans account openings through Governor’s K10,000 initiative
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] By WASITA ROYAL RESIDENTS of East New Britain can now open savings accounts with the ENB Savings and Loans Society without paying the usual K50 fee, thanks to a K10,000 initiative from the Office of the Governor. The East New Britain Provincial Government partnered with ENBSL to disburse the funds,

item.title: ITI hosts 65th graduation ceremony with 394 graduates
item.description: [The PNG Sun] The International Training Institute (ITI) celebrated its 65th Graduation Ceremony with 394 students graduating in various certificate, diploma, and advanced diploma programs at APEC Haus on Friday (16 May). The graduation ceremony was witnessed by proud parents, guardians, families, relatives, spon

item.title: Aropa na Kieta seif long bom nau
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Ol pipel long Central Bougainville i no ken wari tumas long ol bom bilong Wol Woa 2 nau bihain long ABG i wokbung wantaim New Zealand Defence Force long rausim ol.

item.title: Government fails to answer UN concerns on illegal FCA logging and land abuses
item.description: [Act Now PNG] The Papua New Guinea government has failed to answer questions from the United Nations over the misuse of Forest Clearing Authority (FCA) logging licences and controversial land leases.

item.title: Bougainville News Alert : Statement from the Office of the President: Response to Chief John L Momis, GCL, former President of Bougainville
item.description: [Bougainville News] It is disappointing to see a leader of John Momis’ stature, a former two term President of Bougainville and longtime Regional Member for Bougainville trying to undermine the good work of the Autonomous Bougainville Government. See Momis Press Release Part … Continue reading →

item.title: On Starlink and Papua New Guinea’s innovative spirit
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] The issuing of an operator licence to Starlink has generated headlines, but it is far from the only LEO connectivity provider in Papua New Guinea. Meanwhile, the shortlists for the 2026 Innovation PNG Awards have been revealed - and they include exciting small and medium businesses from around the c

item.title: USP Pro-Chancellor challenges new generation of Pacific leadersat Emalus graduation
item.description: [Education News PNG] As The University of the South Pacific (USP) graduation season continues across the region, the Emalus Campus ceremony in Vanuatu today served as a powerful The post USP Pro-Chancellor challenges new generation of Pacific leadersat Emalus graduation first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: Ryan Ani: From Raukele to the Barramandis
item.description: [Inside PNG] A fresh face is joining the PNG Barramandis as they head to Japan for the ICC Men’s T20 World Cup East Asia-Pacific (EAP) Qualifier. At just 22 years old, Ryan Ani is ready to make his international debut. Ani, who hails from Hula and Babaka in the Rigo Coastal area of Central Province, grew up [...

item.title: PNG Hunters fall to Souths Logan Magpies at Santos Stadium
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] A fast-starting Souths Logan Magpies outfit spoiled the PNG Hunters’ home appearance in Port Moresby after claiming a 30-20 victory in the Hostplus Cup on Sunday evening. The Hunters showed flashes of brilliance in attack but defensive lapses proved costly as the Queensland visitors capitalised on t

item.title: Disaster Preparedness Plans in PNG
item.description: [Pawa TV] Papua New Guinea is highly vulnerable to natural disasters due to its geography. Community-based disaster preparedness plans are being implemented, involving local leaders and groups to enhance response and reduce risks. The post Disaster Preparedness Plans in PNG first appeared on PAWA TV .

item.title: PNG NRL Competition finalise Club Line-Up for 2025 Season
item.description: [PNG Buzz] The PNG NRL Competition has announced that the 12 existing teams will compete for the top spot in the 2025 Digicel ExxonMobil Cup, starting in April of 2025. After thoroughly reviewing the 2024 season and assessing the potential for expansion, it was concluded that the competition in 2025 will featu

item.title: PNG PM Heads to France for Climate and Trade Talks with Macron
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea Prime Minister James Marape has travelled to France for official discussions with French President Emmanuel Macron, with climate change, trade and investment expected to dominate the bilateral agenda. PNG PM Heads to France for Climate and Trade Talks with Macron The visit comes as

item.title: Front and Back page
item.description: [Post Courier] FRONT To access this post, you must purchase Web , Web – One-Day , Web – 14-Day , Web – One-Month , Web – Three-Month , Web – Six-Month , Web – Annual , Web & eBook , Web & eBook – One-Day , Web & eBook – 14-Day , Web & eBook – One-Month , Web & eBook – Three-Month , Web & eBook – Six-Month or Web &

item.title: More than 600 recruits commence training at Bomana National Centre of Excellence
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] THE Royal Papua New Guinea Constabulary (RPNGC) on May 18th, 2026, officially commenced training for 662 new recruits at the Bomana National Centre of Excellence (NCoE) in the National Capital District, marking the second intake for 2026. Deputy Commissioner of Police and Chief of Administration, Dr

item.title: PM Marape: PNG maintains among lowest fuel prices globally via government support measures
item.description: [The PNG Sun] PNG Prime Minister, James Marape has assured Papua New Guineans that the Government will continue supporting fuel price stability in the country as global fuel markets remain volatile due to ongoing tensions and conflict in the Middle East. In a statement last week (Friday 15 May), Prime Minister Ma

item.title: Graun bruk katim Sepik Haiwe
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Bikpela ren i mekim graun i bruk na bagarapim rot long Sepik Haiwe. Dispela rot i save bungim Is na Wes Sepik na i go olgeta long boda bilong PNG wantaim Indonesia.

item.title: Government fails to answer UN concerns on illegal FCA logging and land abuses
item.description: [Act Now PNG] The Papua New Guinea government has failed to answer questions from the United Nations over the misuse of Forest Clearing Authority (FCA) logging licences and controversial SABL land leases.

item.title: Bougainville News Alert : A Nation In His Making – A Memory
item.description: [Bougainville News] FIVE YEARS ago we farewelled GC Sir Michael Somare with the most deserving tributes around the country. We also paused to remember him as someone whose political life is synonymous with the birth of PNG as a nation state … Continue reading →

item.title: People Moves: Digicel, CPL Group, Kina Bank, Remington Group, PNG FM, Dentons
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] CEO steps down at Digicel PNG, new chair for CPL Group, new CFO at Kina Bank, first Solomon Islands country head for Remington Group, CEO appointed at PNG FM, new partner at Dentons. The post People Moves: Digicel, CPL Group, Kina Bank, Remington Group, PNG FM, Dentons appeared first on Business Adv

item.title: USP Concludes Laucala Graduation with a strong focus on Pacific Leadership and Legacies
item.description: [Education News PNG] The University of the South Pacific (USP) concluded its two-day graduation ceremonies at the Laucala Campus in Suva, Fiji, today, marking another important milestone as The post USP Concludes Laucala Graduation with a strong focus on Pacific Leadership and Legacies first appeared on Education News P

item.title: Kumuls Legend Alex Johnston Seals Deal with PNG Chiefs
item.description: [Inside PNG] PNG icon Alex Johnston signs with PNG Chiefs for 2028. The NRL try-scoring king joins as the first local hero, honoring his proud Kumuls heritage. The post Kumuls Legend Alex Johnston Seals Deal with PNG Chiefs first appeared on Inside PNG . The post Kumuls Legend Alex Johnston Seals Deal with PNG C

item.title: NZ Police Commissioner Visits PNG to Discuss Regional Policing Partnership
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] New Zealand Police Commissioner Richard Chambers has completed a working visit to Papua New Guinea aimed at strengthening policing cooperation and regional security partnerships between the two countries. NZ Police Commissioner Visits PNG to Discuss Regional Policing Partnership/Photo supplied While

item.title: Greenwashing
item.description: [Pawa TV] Greenwashing misleads the public about environmental responsibility, hinders effective climate action, and damages trust in genuine sustainability efforts, necessitating greater transparency and standardisation in environmental claims. The post Greenwashing first appeared on PAWA TV .

item.title: Operation Render Safe set to begin in East New Britain
item.description: [PNG Buzz] This week, Governor Michael Marum and Provincial Administrator Levi Mano welcomed Australian High Commissioner John Feakes, Defence Adviser Colonel Travis Gordon, and a team from the Australian Defence Force (ADF) in East New Britain. This visit marks the beginning of a series to the province, as th

item.title: Court Rejects PNG Deputy PM Rosso's Attempt to Block Investigation
item.description: [PNG Facts] The National Court has dismissed an application by Papua New Guinea Deputy Prime Minister and Minister for Lands and Physical Planning John Rosso seeking to halt an ongoing investigation by the Ombudsman Commission into allegations relating to undeclared income. Justice Paulus Dowa delivered the rul

item.title: PC Online Tidbits
item.description: [Post Courier] How many times did we predict the PNG Games in Mendi would never happen? Many times, and it's just been scrapped again -for the last time hopefully! Now the Games go to? The post PC Online Tidbits appeared first on Post Courier .

item.title: PM Marape reassures nation on fuel supply and cost-of-living relief measures
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] PRIME Minister James Marape has reassured Papua New Guineans that Government has acted decisively to stabilize fuel supplies and shield citizens from rising Global Fuel Prices caused by international conflict and instability in Global Energy Markets. Addressing media yesterday at Melanesian Haus pri

item.title: Judge Ellis pays courtesy visit to Governor Ipatas’ Office after DCJ Kandakasi’s orders
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Judge Graham Ellis has paid a courtesy visit to Enga Governor Sir Peter Ipatas’ office in Wabag for an hour-long meeting following declaratory orders issued by Deputy Chief Justice Ambeng Kandakasi in relation to a bail application. The orders effectively removed restrictions on Judge Ellis from per

item.title: Gavman Minista bam long kar long haiwe
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Minista bilong 'Energy' na Memba bilong Kairuku, Peter Namea Isoaimo i bin bam long kar long 2nd Februeri, 2026 long Hiritano Haiwe. Em i bam long kar wantaim wanpela soldia bilong PNG Defence Force (PNGDF). Ol kar bilong tupela man i stap nau long polis stesin long 15 Mile na wok painimaut i gohet

item.title: ACT NOW! Spotlights Illegal Logging at Asia Pacific Global Dialogue, Malaysia
item.description: [Act Now PNG] ACT NOW! recently attended and presented on illegal logging and forest crime in Papua New Guinea at the Asia- Pacific Regional Private Sector Dialogue meeting held in Malaysia.

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts : Simon Pentanu : My memories of Kieta
item.description: [Bougainville News] The first permanent German presence in Kieta, Bougainville, began with the establishment of a Marist mission in 1901, followed by an official German colonial administrative office in 1905 to oversee the region. They would have seen, steaming into the deep natural harbour, … Continue reading →

item.title: Meet some of Papua New Guinea’s most innovative small businesses
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] A record number of small businesses entered the 2026 Innovation PNG Awards this year, with the shortlists announced last week in Port Moresby. From the use of artificial intelligence to monitor media reporting to using fly larvae to create poultry feed, we consider the six ingenious projects shortli

item.title: USP honours more than 60 high achievers at Medals and Prizes Ceremony
item.description: [Education News PNG] More than 60 outstanding students and graduates of The University of the South Pacific (USP) were recognised for academic excellence at the Medals and Prizes The post USP honours more than 60 high achievers at Medals and Prizes Ceremony first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: Kina Bank Backs Small Business Innovation for 2026
item.description: [Inside PNG] Kina Bank backs the 2026 Innovation PNG Awards, sponsoring the Small Business category to drive growth and recognize local creative entrepreneurs. The post Kina Bank Backs Small Business Innovation for 2026 first appeared on Inside PNG . The post Kina Bank Backs Small Business Innovation for 2026 ap

item.title: Vladimir Putin to Visit China for Strategic Talks with Xi Jinping
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Russian President Vladimir Putin is set to travel to China next week for high-level talks with Chinese leader Xi Jinping, as Moscow and Beijing continue to reinforce ties amid growing friction with the West. The Kremlin said Putin’s state visit will take place from May 19 to 20 and will focus on exp

item.title: The Malagan Art
item.description: [Pawa TV] Malagan is a ceremonial art tradition from Papua New Guinea, symbolising ancestral spirits and clan identities. Its increasing commercialisation raises concerns over cultural respect, authenticity, and spiritual significance. The post The Malagan Art first appeared on PAWA TV .

item.title: PNG Delegation learns from Madagascar’s Vanilla Industry
item.description: [PNG Buzz] A delegation from Papua New Guinea, including senior officers from central government agencies such as the Department of National Planning and Monitoring (DNPM), the Spice Industry Board (SIB) under the Department of Agriculture and Livestock (DAL), as well as Provincial Government and Administratio

item.title: Bomana academy to get Kiwi boost as NZ police chief visits PNG
item.description: [PNG Facts] A SENIOR New Zealand police officer will soon be deployed to the Bomana National Centre of Excellence following a strategic visit to PNG by Kiwi Police Commissioner Richard Chambers last week. Bomana academy to get Kiwi boost as NZ police chief visits PNG/Photo supplied Hosted by the New Zealand Hig

item.title: New Partnership to Support Finance for Renewable Energy in PNG
item.description: [Post Courier] The National Energy Authority in PNG and the Global Green Growth Institute - GGGI signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) to strengthen cooperation on the mobilization of climate finance for renewable energy development in Papua New Guinea. This cooperation has been made possible thanks to the Pa

item.title: Treasury Minister defends K1 billion contingency funds in PNG budget
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] TREASURER Ian Ling-Stuckey has defended the Government’s allocation of K1 billion in flexible budget funding, arguing that contingency funds are a standard and necessary feature of responsible budgeting. In a statement issued on 18 May, the Treasury Minister said criticism from East Sepik Governor A

item.title: 662 police recruits begin training at Bomana
item.description: [The PNG Sun] A total of 662 police recruits has officially commenced training at the Bomana National Centre of Excellence in Port Moresby, marking the Royal Papua New Guinea Constabulary’s second intake for 2025. Deputy Commissioner of Police and Chief of Administration, Dr Philip Mitna, officiated at the openin

item.title: Dei bai-ileksen suruk bek tupela de
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] PNGEC i wari long hevi bilong sekyuriti na i surukim bai-ileksen bilong Dei Open i go bek tupela de

item.title: Fergusson islanders demand compensation for 'unauthorised FCA logging'
item.description: [Act Now PNG] Communities on Fergusson Island are demanding compensation for environmental damage and unpaid royalties from logging operations they say were carried out without their consent.

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts : President Toroama confirms ABG position on Panguna Partnering process
item.description: [Bougainville News] President Toroama confirms ABG position on Panguna Partnering process The President of the Autonomous Bougainville Government (ABG), Hon. Ishmael Toroama, has confirmed that the ABG has rejected the proposed partnership between Bougainville Copper Limited (BCL) and CMOC Group Limited in … Continue r

item.title: Papua New Guinea retailers outline substantial capex programs
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Business is back and better than ever for Papua New Guinea's leading retailers after recovering from the post-10 January downturn. As a result, many are now planning substantial investments to support longer-term growth and diversification, as CEOs tell Business Advantage PNG . The post Papua New Gu

item.title: Australia Awards offers development grants for colleges
item.description: [Education News PNG] Teachers and nursing colleges in Papua New Guinea (PNG) have the opportunity to apply for Institutional Development Grants (IDG) up to K150,000.00 from Australia Awards. The post Australia Awards offers development grants for colleges first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: Kina Bank’s Rangeview Digital Hub Returns with Level 1 Upgrade
item.description: [Inside PNG] Kina Bank reopens its upgraded Rangeview Digital Hub on Level 1, bringing instant cards, loans, and digital support to Port Moresby shoppers. The post Kina Bank’s Rangeview Digital Hub Returns with Level 1 Upgrade first appeared on Inside PNG . The post Kina Bank’s Rangeview Digital Hub Returns with

item.title: China Hands Over US$1 Million Aid Package to PNG Government
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] PNG has been boosted with a US$1 million humanitarian assistance package from China as both nations reaffirmed their long-standing diplomatic partnership during a ceremony in Port Moresby. China Hands Over US$1 Million Aid Package to PNG Government/Photo supplied Chinese Embassy Chargé d'affaires a.

item.title: Papua New Guinea’s Round Log Export Policy and Its Benefits
item.description: [Pawa TV] Papua New Guinea plans to phase out raw log exports by 2025, promoting local processing to boost economic growth, job creation, and sustainable forest management, potentially increasing forestry revenue significantly. The post Papua New Guinea’s Round Log Export Policy and Its Benefits first appeare

item.title: Bougainville chocolate festival 2023 underway
item.description: [PNG Buzz] The 2023 annual Bougainville Chocolate Festival is now underway, held over three days from the 26 to 28 September at the Hutjena Oval in Buka. It will be the largest event since the inaugural festival in 2016. The theme of this year’s festival is “Innovation and Information Sharing to Empower Farmer

item.title: PNG Chiefs Eye Connor Watson After Surprise Port Moresby Visit
item.description: [PNG Facts] PNG’s newest NRL franchise has intensified its recruitment drive after Sydney Roosters utility Connor Watson touched down in Port Moresby on Sunday for high-level discussions with the PNG Chiefs. Watson arrived alongside partner Kiara less than 24 hours after the Roosters’ Magic Round defeat to the

item.title: PNG Parliament holds meeting with Chinese Embassy officials
item.description: [Post Courier] THE PAPUA New Guinea Parliament has held an advance courtesy meeting with representatives from the Embassy of the People’s Republic of China ahead of a senior Chinese delegation visit later this week. The meeting involved Kala Aufa, Clerk of Parliament, and Pang Hanzhao, Chargé d’Affaires and Minist

item.title: Commissioning of first ambulance in Unggai-Bena District with more to come
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] THE National St John Ambulance Council Papua New Guinea (NStJA) is proud to announce the commissioning of the first ambulance in Unggai-Bena District, Eastern Highlands Province. Through the request and funding support of the Unggai-Bena District Development Authority (DDA), in partnership with the

item.title: PM Marape assures public PNGDF investigation into recruitment continues
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Prime Minister James Marape says investigations into the Papua New Guinea Defense Force are actively continuing despite limited public updates on the matter. Speaking to the media yesterday afternoon before departing for France on official duties at the Melanesian House in Port Moresby, Mr. Marape a

item.title: PMGH laik ol famili rausim bodi long mog hariap
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Sif Ekseketiv Opisa bilong Pot Mosbi Jeneral Haus Sik, Dokta Paki Molumi i singaut long ol pipel long go sekim mog na rausim dai bodi bilong ol famili na wantok bilong ol hariap, na karim i go planim. Mog i pulap na wanem ol bodi i stap longtaim bai haus sik i bungim olgeta na planim long wanpela bi

item.title: Bougainville landowners warn companies at Panguna mine to stop work
item.description: [Act Now PNG] A group of landowners in the area of the Panguna mine in Bougainville has issued a public warning to companies trying to redevelop the mine to stop work and leave.

item.title: Bougainville News Alert 2026 : Simon Pentanu : My meeting with Francis Ona
item.description: [Bougainville News] Meeting Francis Ona – from my Panguna journals. My six year appointment as Chief Ombudsman that began in January 1995 ended on 31 December 2000. This time was also the end of my national public service career. It was … Continue reading →

item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Papua New Guinea's long-awaited entry into the National Rugby League is slowly taking shape. At the centre of the journey is the newly appointed Chief Executive Officer of the PNG Chiefs. She spoke with Michael Philip. item.description: [Education News PNG] The Manus Provincial Education Board (PEB) has issued a delay notice for the start of term 2 due to severe weather. In a memorandum to The post Term 2 Delay in Manus first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: National Court Overturns Ombudsman Order Blocking Starlink Licence in PNG
item.description: [Inside PNG] PNG’s National Court quashes the Ombudsman Commission’s "ultra vires" order, clearing the legal path for NICTA to resume Starlink’s satellite license review. The post National Court Overturns Ombudsman Order Blocking Starlink Licence in PNG first appeared on Inside PNG . The post National Court Over

item.title: PNG Prime Minister Marape Hails Solomon Islands Prime Minister Election Outcome
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Prime Minister James Marape says Papua New Guinea is ready to deepen cooperation with the Solomon Islands after the election of Matthew Wale as the Pacific nation’s new Prime Minister. Mr Wale was voted into office by Parliament in Honiara on Friday, prompting messages of support from regional leade

item.title: Volcanic Lakes in PNG
item.description: [Pawa TV] Papua New Guinea's volcanic lakes, formed by ancient eruptions, offer tourism opportunities and support local economies and ecosystems. Sustainable management can enhance eco-tourism while preserving these unique natural treasures. The post Volcanic Lakes in PNG first appeared on PAWA TV .

item.title: Agripreneurs empowered, MSMEs boosted in Sepik Region
item.description: [PNG Buzz] Under the EU-STREIT PNG programme, FAO equips local entrepreneurs with knowledge, skills and practical tools needed to drive inclusive sustainable development for MSMEs in the Sepik region. Wewak, Papua New Guinea – The Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO), as part of the EU

item.title: PNG PM Marape Threatens Sackings Over Fuel Subsidy Payment Delays
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea Prime Minister James Marape has demanded urgent action to resolve delays in fuel subsidy payments, warning that government officials responsible for holdups could lose their jobs as fuel supply disruptions continue across the country. Several service stations have reportedly faced t

item.title: Gold and silver confiscated from Australian passenger at Jacksons International Airport
item.description: [Post Courier] On Thursday, 14th May 2026, Aviation Security Officers detected suspicious items belonging to an Australian citizen traveling on a Qantas Flight from Port Moresby to Brisbane. Two separate detections were made: To access this post, you must purchase Web , Web – One-Day , Web – 14-Day , Web – One-Mon

item.title: SEZA and ICSA sign MoU to strengthen information sharing and investor support in SEZs
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] By SEPKOLIN WALNE THE Special Economic Zones Authorities (SEZA) and the Immigration and Citizenship Services Authorities (ICSA) has formally signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) last week Friday to strengthen institutional cooperation through structured information sharing and administrative c

item.title: PM Marape departs for France on official duties
item.description: [The PNG Sun] PNG Prime Minister James Marape departed for France on official duties yesterday afternoon. Mr. Marape addressed the media at his Melanesian Haus office in Port Moresby yesterday before his trip. Related news: https://www.postcourier.com.pg/pm-marape-to-strengthen-png-france-relations-during-officia

item.title: Australia pasim tingting bilong PNG wantaim ragbi lig
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Australia i bilip ol i pasim tingting na bel bilong Papaua Niugini pinis taim ol i sapotim PNG long putim wanpela tim insait long NRL. Ol i bilip dispela i pasim PNG na i no wari long ol arapela wokbung PNG i gat wantaim ol arapela kantri olsem China.

item.title: New forestry sector Risk Alert highlights compliance risks linked to KK Connections Group
item.description: [Act Now PNG] ACT NOW! has published a new Risk Alert examining the compliance risk environment surrounding one of Papua New Guinea’s largest forestry operators.

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts :Rio Tinto urged to accelerate action on remediation of Panguna mine disaster, one year on from investigation
item.description: [Bougainville News] One year on from the release of an independent investigation into Rio Tinto’s former Panguna mine in Bougainville, communities living with the ongoing environmental impact are calling on the company to urgently move towards funding solutions, particularly in areas identified … Continue reading →

item.title: Port Moresby’s newest shopping mall opens its doors
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] The opening of the Eliseo Premium, a five-storey retail and entertainment complex in Hohola, reflects growing business confidence in Papua New Guinea’s capital. The post Port Moresby’s newest shopping mall opens its doors appeared first on Business Advantage PNG .

item.title: USP commits $1 million in student support amid global crisis
item.description: [Education News PNG] The University of the South Pacific (USP) has today announced a $F1 million student hardship and bursary support package to protect students and their families The post USP commits $1 million in student support amid global crisis first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: Bula Round? How Fiji can host Super Rugby’s big weekend
item.description: [Inside PNG] Christchurch's Super Round rebirth will give rugby a timely boost as rival destinations, including Fiji, jostle for a piece of the action. The post Bula Round? How Fiji can host Super Rugby’s big weekend first appeared on Inside PNG . The post Bula Round? How Fiji can host Super Rugby’s big weekend

item.title: PNG's Central Province Builds Closer Links With Cook Islands
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Historic ties between PNG Central Province and the Cook Islands were reinforced on Tuesday with the signing of a Sister Province Partnership Arrangement aimed at deepening cooperation between the two island communities. The agreement was signed at APEC Haus by Central Governor Rufina Peter and Cook

item.title: Know Your Rights – Absolute & Qualified
item.description: [Pawa TV] The PNG Constitution protects both absolute and qualified rights, with absolute rights being inviolable. Qualified rights can be restricted by law, raising concerns about accountability and governance, especially during the COVID-19 pandemic. The post Know Your Rights – Absolute & Qualified first ap

item.title: Showcasing PNG to the world: 2023 Goroka Show a Success
item.description: [PNG Buzz] Papua New Guinea’s rich cultural heritage came alive once again as the 67th Goroka Show took centre stage at the National Sports Institute (NSI) in Goroka, Eastern Highlands Province. Held over the long weekend commemorating PNG’s 48th Independence, this year’s event, sponsored by the National Gamin

item.title: Ombudsman Commission Probes PNG Deputy PM Rosso for Alleged Corruption
item.description: [PNG Facts] Allegations of misconduct linked to income declarations made before entering Parliament have placed Papua New Guinea Deputy Prime Minister John Rosso under investigation by the Ombudsman Commission. Ombudsman Commission Probes PNG Deputy PM Rosso for Alleged Corruption The senior government minister

item.title: Court orders weapons ban amid Kandep tribal warfare
item.description: [Post Courier] The National Court of Justice sitting at Wabag has intervened in the escalating tribal conflict in Kandep, issuing a sweeping set of interim orders aimed at restoring public safety and law and order in the troubled district. Presiding Judge, Justice Graham Ellis, handed down the directives on Tuesda

item.title: PMGH working with DPM to secure permanent positions for nurses
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] PORT Moresby General Hospital (PMGH) Chief Executive Officer, Dr. Paki Molumi, has announced that the hospital is working closely with the Department of Personnel Management (DPM) to transition nurses into permanent public service positions under the Alesco payroll system. Dr. Molumi confirmed that

item.title: Supreme Court dismisses Opposition’s challenge against Vote of No Confidence Law
item.description: [The PNG Sun] The Pangu-led Marape-Rosso Government will continue to serve its full tenure in office following the Supreme Court which has dismissed a constitutional challenge against Papua New Guinea’s amended Vote of No Confidence (VONC) law. In a 23-page verdict, the court has effectively upheld the controvers

item.title: K30.9 bilien baset
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Gavman i tokaut long K30.9 bilien baset bilong kantri long 2026 tasol em bai no inap yet long bungim ol nid na laik bilong ol manmeri.

item.title: New forestry sector Risk Alert highlights compliance risks linked to KK Connections Group
item.description: [Act Now PNG] ACT NOW! has published a new Risk Alert examining the compliance risk environment surrounding the KK Connections corporate group, one of PNG’s largest forestry operators.

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts : OBEC returns 45 writs, marks completion of 2025 ABG General Election
item.description: [Bougainville News] Bougainville Electoral Commissioner, Desmond Tsianai, today formally returned 45 writs comprising the Presidency, 38 single-member constituencies and the six reserved regional seats for women and Former Combatants. Tsianai formally handed the election writs to the outgoing Speaker, Simon Pentanu, of

item.title: Moni Plus MD on standing out in Papua New Guinea’s competitive financial services market
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Financial institution Heduru Moni Limited, trading as Moni Plus, has been on an impressive growth trajectory in recent years. In this exclusive interview with Business Advantage PNG , its Managing Director Aho Baliki expands on its long-term strategy and shares what it is doing to stand out in Papua

item.title: USP study provides baseline data on Fiji’s drinking water
item.description: [Education News PNG] As the world marks World Water Day 2026 under the theme “Water and Gender Equality,” research from The University of the South Pacific (USP) has The post USP study provides baseline data on Fiji’s drinking water first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: How extreme Aussie weather is fuelling ‘eco-anxiety’
item.description: [Inside PNG] More than half of Australians say they are giving up on regular hobbies such as gardening and playing sport due to stress brought on by extreme weather. The post How extreme Aussie weather is fuelling ‘eco-anxiety’ first appeared on Inside PNG . The post How extreme Aussie weather is fuelling ‘eco-a

item.title: PNG Opposition Raises Alarm Over Chief Justice Referral
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Papua New Guinea Deputy Opposition Leader Keith Iduhu has questioned the Ombudsman Commission’s decision to refer Chief Justice Sir Gibbs Salika to the National Executive Council, warning that the move could undermine the country’s constitutional democracy. Speaking alongside Opposition Leader James

item.title: The Challenge of Maritime Security
item.description: [Pawa TV] Papua New Guinea's maritime security is crucial due to its rich marine resources and threats from illegal fishing. Enhanced surveillance, training, and international cooperation are vital for protecting these waters. The post The Challenge of Maritime Security first appeared on PAWA TV .

item.title: Hambini Village’s journey to transformation
item.description: [PNG Buzz] Thanks to community unity, international support by the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO) and visionary leadership, 9,000+ people of Hambini Village in East Sepik Province not only took a path to improve their economic prospects using cocoa but also paved the way for a br

item.title: PNG Receives US$1 Million Humanitarian Support from China
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea has received US$1 million in humanitarian cash assistance from the Chinese Government following a handover ceremony attended by Prime Minister James Marape and senior government officials in Port Moresby. PNG Receives US$1 Million Humanitarian Support from China/Photo supplied The s

item.title: NICTA enforces internet content filtering directive
item.description: [Post Courier] The National Information and Communication Technology Authority (NICTA) has activated regulatory measures to enforce unsuitable internet content filtering in line with a directive from the ICT Minister. On Tuesday, May 19, NICTA announced that it is delivering multi-category enforcement on a phased

item.title: Marape welcomes NRL star Connor Watson
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] PRIME Minister James Marape has warmly welcomed current Sydney Roosters and NRL representative player Connor Watson to Papua New Guinea during his visit to Port Moresby to tour PNG Chiefs facilities and explore potential opportunities with the Franchise ahead of its historic entry into the National

item.title: Port Moresby to host Pacific Community Day this Saturday
item.description: [The PNG Sun] National Capital District Governor Powes Parkop has announced that Port Moresby will host the Pacific Community Day this Saturday, May 16, from 12pm to 9pm at Sir Hubert Murray Stadium, with major local and regional artists set to perform. Speaking during the official announcement yesterday at Sir H

item.title: La Nina bai kam - gavman mas redi
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] National Weather Service (NWS) bilong PNG i tok 'La Nina' bai kam klostu nau tasol i no gat wanpela toksave i kam long gavman yet long wanem samting ol bai mekim o sapos ol i redi long wanem kain ol hevi dispela taim nogut bai nap long bringim.

item.title: ACT NOW! backs Parliamentary Committee call for immediate and drastic reform in forest sector
item.description: [Act Now PNG] ACT NOW! supports the call from the Special Parliamentary Committee on Public Sector Reform for ‘immediate and nonnegotiable structural, punitive and legislative reforms’ in the forestry sector.

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts : Could tourism lead Bougainville into the future ?
item.description: [Bougainville News] Bougainville has gone through many turbulent periods over the past 100 years, not least the Civil War of the 1990s, which claimed as many as 20,000 lives. The government is now looking to develop the economy and has its eye … Continue reading →

item.title: Papua New Guinea banking enters its digital age
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Papua New Guinea's banks are scaling up digital platforms to fuel growth and advance financial inclusion, as CEOs in the sector tell Business Advantage PNG . The post Papua New Guinea banking enters its digital age appeared first on Business Advantage PNG .

item.title: Call for Applications for Membership of the National Higher and Technical Education Board (NHTEB)
item.description: [Education News PNG] 88676120 Download The post Call for Applications for Membership of the National Higher and Technical Education Board (NHTEB) first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: Ok Tedi Welcomes New Graduates to 2026 Development Scheme
item.description: [Inside PNG] Ok Tedi Mining Limited welcomes 44 graduates into its 2026 development program, including seven top scholars from Unitech for a future-ready PNG. The post Ok Tedi Welcomes New Graduates to 2026 Development Scheme first appeared on Inside PNG . The post Ok Tedi Welcomes New Graduates to 2026 Developm

item.title: Fuel Prices in PNG to Rise as Suppliers Return to Market Rates
item.description: [PNG Facts] Motorists and businesses across Papua New Guinea are expected to pay more for fuel after suppliers announced they would return to market-based pricing following issues with the Government’s fuel subsidy arrangements. The Papua New Guinea Chamber of Resources and Energy (PNG CORE), which represents f

item.title: ICCC only regulates allowable margins for Petrol, Diesel and Kerosene
item.description: [Post Courier] The Independent Consumer and Competition Commission (ICCC) have direct regulatory oversight on the allowable margins for Petrol, Diesel, and Kerosene, which are declared petroleum products under the Prices Regulation. Act (Chapter 320), pursuant to National Gazette No. G548 dated 8 August 2016 issue

item.title: Kokopo hosts final validation for oil palm bills
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] By GEORGINA MICHAEL The oil palm industry corporation held its second regional validation workshop on the proposed oil palm Management Bill 2025 and the Oil palm Authority Bill 2025 in Kokopo last week. The validation program was witnessed by the Governor of East New Britain, Mr. Michael Marum, the

item.title: Timor-Leste President Jose Ramos-Horta plans to set up Embassy in PNG
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Papua New Guinea and Timor-Leste are planning to establish diplomatic missions in each country. The plan was announced by both PNG Prime Minister James Marape and President of Timor-Leste His Excellency José Ramos-Horta during a joint media conference in Port Moresby this week. Related news: https:/

item.title: ACT NOW! backs Parliamentary Committee call for immediate and drastic forest sector reform
item.description: [Act Now PNG] ACT NOW! supports the call from the Special Parliamentary Committee on Public sector Reform for ‘immediate and nonnegotiable structural, punitive and legislative reforms’ in the forestry sector.

item.title: Independence 1 September 2027 :Opening Statement by ABG President Hon. Ishmael Toroama, MHR at the Joint Consultation Moderator Meeting
item.description: [Bougainville News] Opening Statement by ABG President Hon. Ishmael Toroama, MHR at the Joint Consultation Moderator Meeting Sir Jerry Mateparae, Distinguished Moderator, the Honourable James Marape, Prime Minister of Papua New Guinea, Foreign Missions, distinguished dignitaries, ladies and gentlemen. item.title: Loloata unveils new food offering
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Mary Tao investigates the new food offerings at Loloata Island Resort under their new head chef. The post Loloata unveils new food offering appeared first on Business Advantage PNG .

item.title: Pacific Academy Unveils AI-Powered Research Platform
item.description: [Education News PNG] APIA, SAMOA – 16 February 2026 – The Pacific Academy of Sciences (PAS) today announced two historic milestones for Pacific research and innovation at its The post Pacific Academy Unveils AI-Powered Research Platform first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: ABG launches Bougainville Wings, region’s new flag carrier
item.description: [Inside PNG] The Autonomous Bougainville Government has launched Bougainville Wings Limited, describing it as Bougainville’s flag carrier and a milestone for the region’s aviation sector. The post ABG launches Bougainville Wings, region’s new flag carrier first appeared on Inside PNG . The post ABG launches Boug

item.title: BSP Team Brings Graduate Program Awareness to PNG's Divine Word University
item.description: [PNG Facts] Students at Divine Word University in Madang have been encouraged to prepare early for employment opportunities after Bank South Pacific conducted a Graduate Development Program awareness session on campus. The roadshow gave aspiring graduates an opportunity to engage directly with BSP officials and

item.title: ICCC explains regulatory oversight on fuel pricing
item.description: [Post Courier] The Independent Consumer and Competition Commission (ICCC) commend the Government’s bold policy decision to cushion fuel supply shock on domestic fuel prices through a subsidy mechanism. The ICCC is part of the High-Level State Taskforce team on fuel crisis, that collaborates with other key agencies

item.title: Ok Tedi’s Misima Minerals strengthens Government Partnerships through support to Misima PNG Correctional Services
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] Misima Minerals Limited (Misima Minerals) is committed to working closely with the communities on the island. The company, a wholly owned subsidiary of Ok Tedi Mining Limited (Ok Tedi) demonstrated this recently through the delivery of food supplies to the Misima Papua New Guinea Correctional Servic

item.title: Kaupa rejects claims over vehicles found in Goroka
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Moresby North-East MP, John Kaupa, has denied allegations that vehicles linked to the Port Moresby North-East District were found operating in Goroka, Eastern Highlands. He said the claims were false and misleading. Speaking during a media conference on Monday, Mr. Kaupa said the vehicles involved w

item.title: Parliament Committee highly critical of PNG Forest Authority and the logging industry
item.description: [Act Now PNG] The Special Parliamentary Committee on Public Sector Reform has published a report that is scathing in its criticism of the PNG Forest Authority and the logging industry.

item.title: PNG PM Marape Congratulates New Solomon Islands Leader Matthew Wale
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea Prime Minister James Marape has congratulated newly elected Solomon Islands Prime Minister Matthew Wale following his successful election by Parliament in Honiara yesterday. Mr Wale secured the top political post after Members of Parliament in the Solomon Islands voted to elect him

item.title: Forestry Sector Money Laundering Risk Assessment presents a devastating picture of corruption
item.description: [Act Now PNG] A Money Laundering Risk Assessment for the Forestry Sector published by the Bank of Papua New Guinea presents a devastating picture of large-scale corruption, fraud and illegality in the logging industry.

item.title: PNG PM Marape Hails Supreme Court Ruling as Win for Stability
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea Prime Minister James Marape has welcomed the Supreme Court’s decision upholding the constitutional amendment on motions of no confidence, describing the ruling as a major victory for political stability in the country. The Supreme Court dismissed a challenge against Constitutional A
